title: Monospora trinervis
taxonomy: Monospora trinervis belongs to the family of fungi, specifically the genus Monospora. The species Monospora trinervis is often classified as an anamorphic fungus, which means its asexual spores are the dominant form of reproduction. This classification indicates that Monospora trinervis primarily reproduces through asexual means, producing spores for dispersal. The exact classification may vary depending on the source, but it is generally categorized within the kingdom Fungi. Further research may be necessary to provide a more comprehensive classification.
